question:
Will Bitcoin reach $125,000 in November?

Description: This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

evidences.title: What To Expect From Bitcoin Price In November 2025
evidences.content: ## Bitcoin Is Establishing Crucial Levels On-chain data adds another layer of insight into Bitcoin's current position. The Cost Basis Distribution Heatmap highlights significant support of around $111,000 and notable supply pressure of nearly $117,000. This range defines the battleground between recent buyers looking to defend positions and profit-takers seeking exits after the recent rally. A breakout in either direction could determine the trajectory for the coming weeks. If bulls manage to push beyond the $117,000 supply zone, momentum could accelerate sharply. Conversely, a failure to hold $111,000 could shift sentiment bearish, prompting short-term corrections. Bitcoin Cost Basis Distribution Heatmap Bitcoin Cost Basis Distribution Heatmap. Source: Glassnode [...] > "Global trade tensions, inflation, and recession fears have weighed heavily on all risk assets, and Bitcoin hasn't been spared. We've seen it trade in that $104,000 to $108,000 range recently. Looking ahead, I think November will likely bring consolidation or a modest recovery — not a full-on rally unless a strong catalyst appears. If trade tensions worsen, Bitcoin could retest the $90,000 area. But if support h...

evidences.title: [05 November 2025] That Red Thin Line: Bitcoin Breaks Key ...
evidences.content: Search For Summary Chart Dashboard Query Pro Chart Quicktake Research Creator Popular search exchange flows BTC miner flows derivatives utxo View all results ↵ Navigate ↓ ↑ Open in new tab ctrl ↵ Close Esc BTC Long-term Short-term Technical Analysis On-chain Analysis Sentiment Analysis # [05 November 2025] That Red Thin Line: Bitcoin Breaks Key Support Level That Confirmed the 2022 Bear Market Executive Summary In previous reports, we discussed how Bitcoin fundamentals had weakened, leading to a correction towards the low $100K levels. Indeed, CryptoQuant’s Bull Score Index has fallen to zero (extremely bearish) for the first time since June 2022. [...] The Traders’ On-chain Realized Price. The Traders’ On-chain Realized Price Bands suggest Bitcoin could decline towards $72K (min band) in a period of 1-2 months if the price doesn’t reclaim the lower band quickly ($100K). So far this bull cycle, the Trader Realized Price lower band has been a support for the price of Bitcoin, as traders realize losses by selling. However, if enough selling pressure emerges, the next price support would be the Traders’ minimum price band, which sits at $72K today. [...] As fundamentals remain soft...

evidences.title: MicroStrategy Stock and Bitcoin 2025: Dive into Bitcoin's Impact
evidences.content: MicroStrategy has made headlines with its bold investment strategy in Bitcoin. Consequently, investors are wondering about MicroStrategy’s stock predictions for 2025. Predictions for MicroStrategy stock in 2025 show high variability. Bitcoin holdings heavily influence them. MarketBeat suggests a price range from $409 to $650. The average is $536.90. Stock Analysis forecasts an average target price of $382.10, with a potential reach of up to $650. Analysts are generally optimistic about MicroStrategy’s future performance in 2025. The company’s aggressive Bitcoin investments have a significant impact on its stock predictions. MicroStrategy’s pricing is closely tied to the volatile cryptocurrency market. Since starting Bitcoin acquisitions in 2020, the MicroStrategy chart has shown a 2169% [...] ### Insights from Industry Experts Analysts remain divided on MicroStrategy’s prospects. evidences.title: Strategy Inc vs. BlackRock: Which is the better Bitcoin ...
evidences.content: As of mid‑2025, Strategy holds approximately 580,250 BTC, solidifying its position as the largest corporate Bitcoin holder globally. Did you know? Strategy holds more Bitcoin than most countries. In fact, it holds more than all sovereign nations except the US, China and the UK. What is BlackRock’s Bitcoin ETF stock? Now let’s turn to BlackRock, whose entry into the Bitcoin market brought the world’s largest asset manager into direct competition with long-time crypto natives. In January 2024, after years of US SEC resistance, the regulator approved a slate of spot Bitcoin ETFs. BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) was among them. [...] Strategy is expected to keep adding Bitcoin to its balance sheet, continuing its high-conviction, high-leverage approach. The company’s “Bitcoin capital allocation strategy” includes further debt and equity issuance, meaning future performance will remain tightly tied to BTC price action and potentially vulnerable to margin pressure. That said, institutional support is growing: BlackRock now owns over 5% of Strategy’s stock, signaling confidence in its long-term thesis. IBIT’s path is cleaner and more scalable. After its record-breaking launch...

evidences.title: Bitcoin in 2025: Analyst Forecasts and the IBIT ETF Deep Dive | Saxo
evidences.content: #### 1. Historical Performance Correlation with Bitcoin: IBIT closely mirrors bitcoin’s price movements due to its spot-based structure. This means that as bitcoin’s price fluctuates, the ETF moves in near lockstep, making it a highly efficient proxy for the underlying asset. Recent Performance: On January 6, 2025, IBIT closed at $58.18, up 3.96% for the day. This rise aligns closely with bitcoin’s price increase on the same day, driven by optimism around institutional adoption and regulatory clarity. #### 2. Liquidity and Volume IBIT benefits from strong liquidity, averaging 48.61M shares traded daily, which ensures minimal price slippage for large trades. On January 6, it recorded a volume of 46.72M shares, indicating consistent investor interest. #### 3. Expense Ratio [...] Bernstein Analysts: Predict bitcoin could reach $200,000, citing strong inflows into spot ETFs and institutional adoption. James Butterfill (CoinShares): Projects a range of $80,000 to $150,000 for 2025, with a long-term outlook of $250,000, should bitcoin capture 25% of gold’s market capitalization. Matrixport: Forecasts bitcoin rising to $160,000, driven by favorable economic conditions and ETF demand. V...

evidences.content: import we are working on is a dataf frame. So the data frame columns can be defined. They are going to be the result that we have seen previously on the documentation and then the data frame by using the obtained response and the defined columns. Now it is time to convert each column into its respective type because the values are strings by default. This way open, high, low, close and volume will be defined as float. [music] Start and end time will be defined as datetime and human readable. At the end, we have to return our new data frame. It is time to test our new function to see if it's working. Let's create some test variables and store the result in two [music] so we can print it to see the result. When we use two string in a dataf frame variable, we are converting to a string with [...] data sets. Let's insert the endpoint [music] method and the dictionary of mandatory parameters. Then we can insert the optional parameters. But we have to be careful. The limit variable is mutable. Its value depends of the max API limit.
